entries.title: Thrawn Returns as Strategic Imperial Threat

content.fact: Grand Admiral Thrawn returns from the Unknown Regions and turns fragmented Imperial forces into a coherent strategic threat.
content.injection: During the Thrawn campaign, write the Imperial threat as disciplined, art-informed, and strategically coordinated under Thrawn rather than brute-force warlord chaos.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Thrawn Returns.
Respect story-position bounds from Thrawn Returns to Thrawn Returns.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

entries.title: Thrawn Uses Art as Strategic Intelligence

content.fact: Thrawn studies art and culture to infer enemy psychology, doctrine, and likely decisions.
content.injection: When Thrawn is active, let him exploit cultural analysis and pattern recognition; do not reduce him to generic villain arrogance.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Thrawn Campaign.
Respect story-position bounds from Thrawn Returns to Thrawn Defeated.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Pellaeon Is a Professional Imperial Witness

content.fact: Gilad Pellaeon serves as Thrawn’s experienced subordinate and provides a disciplined Imperial military viewpoint.
content.injection: Use Pellaeon as professional, pragmatic, and observant, not as a Sith fanatic or careless thug. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Thrawn Campaign.
Respect story-position bounds from Thrawn Returns to Thrawn Defeated.

entries.title: C’baoth Is Unstable Dark Jedi, Not Sith Lord

content.fact: Joruus C’baoth is an unstable clone dark Jedi used by Thrawn, not a formal Sith master.
content.injection: In Thrawn Trilogy context, C’baoth can be powerful and dangerous, but do not portray him as Palpatine’s direct Sith successor.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Heir to the Empire Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Heir to the Empire Crisis to Heir to the Empire Crisis.

entries.title: Mara Jade’s Emperor’s Hand Past Is Hidden

content.fact: Mara Jade was once the Emperor’s Hand, but that past is not public knowledge and carries lethal emotional and political consequences.
content.injection: Before Mara’s history is revealed in-scene, do not let casual characters know she served Palpatine; treat it as dangerous hidden identity material.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Thrawn Campaign.
Respect story-position bounds from Thrawn Returns to Thrawn Defeated.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Mara Carries the Kill Luke Command

content.fact: Mara carries Palpatine’s last command to kill Luke, complicating her motives even when she cooperates with New Republic heroes.
content.injection: During early Mara/Luke interactions, preserve tension: she may cooperate with Luke while carrying a compelled or obsessive command to kill him.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Thrawn Campaign.
Respect story-position bounds from Thrawn Returns to Thrawn Defeated.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Karrde Is an Information Broker, Not Rebel Loyalist

content.fact: Talon Karrde’s organization values information, neutrality, and survival before open allegiance to the New Republic.
content.injection: Write Karrde as measured, commercially minded, and protective of his network; his cooperation should feel negotiated, not automatic heroism.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Thrawn Campaign.
Respect story-position bounds from Thrawn Returns to Thrawn Defeated.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

content.fact: Ysalamiri can create local bubbles where Force use is suppressed, making them strategically valuable against Jedi.
content.injection: In Thrawn Campaign, where ysalamiri are present, block easy Force sensing, telekinesis, mind influence, and Jedi combat assumptions inside their effect.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Thrawn Campaign.
Respect story-position bounds from Thrawn Returns to Thrawn Defeated.

entries.title: Noghri Loyalty Turns on Imperial Deception

content.fact: The Noghri serve Vader’s legacy and the Empire under a manipulated debt until Leia exposes the truth of Honoghr’s poisoning.
content.injection: Before the Noghri truth is revealed, write them as lethal Imperial-aligned commandos; after it, their loyalty can pivot toward Leia and her family.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Noghri Truth Revealed.
Respect story-position bounds from Noghri Truth Revealed to Noghri Truth Revealed.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Leia’s Vader Lineage Matters to Noghri

content.fact: Leia’s status as Vader’s daughter is central to how the Noghri interpret her authority and obligations.
content.injection: In Noghri contexts after the reveal, let Leia’s Vader lineage carry cultural and political weight rather than only personal discomfort.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Noghri Truth Revealed.
Respect story-position bounds from Noghri Truth Revealed to Noghri Truth Revealed.

entries.title: Katana Fleet Is a Strategic Force Multiplier

content.fact: The Katana Fleet can rapidly shift military balance because it offers many lost warships to whoever recovers them.
content.injection: During the Katana Fleet race, make the objective about strategic force multiplication, not mere treasure hunting.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Katana Fleet Race.
Respect story-position bounds from Katana Fleet Race to Katana Fleet Race.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Mount Tantiss Is a Hidden Cloning and Storehouse Site

content.fact: Mount Tantiss on Wayland hides Imperial cloning resources and secrets that Thrawn exploits.
content.injection: Before Mount Tantiss is discovered, do not let New Republic characters casually know the source of Thrawn’s cloning advantage.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Heir to the Empire Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Heir to the Empire Crisis to Heir to the Empire Crisis.

entries.title: Thrawn Cloning Is a New Crisis, Not Clone Wars Repeat

content.fact: Thrawn’s use of cloning technology is a specific hidden tactical advantage, not a public return to Clone Wars-style clone armies.
content.injection: During the Thrawn campaign, keep cloning secretive and tied to Mount Tantiss/Spaarti technology unless the context has exposed it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Thrawn Campaign.
Respect story-position bounds from Thrawn Returns to Thrawn Defeated.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Leia Pregnant with Twins During Thrawn Campaign

content.fact: During much of the Thrawn campaign, Leia is pregnant with Jacen and Jaina while carrying major diplomatic and political burdens.
content.injection: In Thrawn-era scenes, Leia’s pregnancy with twins can affect risk, Noghri attention, and political pressure; do not replace her agency with passivity.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Thrawn Campaign.
Respect story-position bounds from Thrawn Returns to Thrawn Defeated.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Jacen and Jaina Birth Changes Solo Family State

content.fact: The birth of Jacen and Jaina changes Han and Leia’s obligations and creates new targets for Imperial and dark-side threats.
content.injection: After the twins are born, include childcare, protection, political security, and family vulnerability when relevant.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Thrawn Defeated.
Respect story-position bounds from Thrawn Defeated to Thrawn Defeated.

entries.title: Luke and Mara Are Not Romantic Yet

content.fact: During the Thrawn campaign, Luke and Mara’s bond is tense, complex, and formative, but not yet their later romance or marriage.
content.injection: Do not portray Luke and Mara as already romantic partners in Thrawn Trilogy context; use distrust, reluctant alliance, and unresolved history.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Thrawn Campaign.
Respect story-position bounds from Thrawn Returns to Thrawn Defeated.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Thrawn’s Defeat Does Not End Imperial Threats

content.fact: Thrawn’s defeat breaks his campaign but does not eliminate Imperial warlords, dark-side remnants, or future crises.
content.injection: After Thrawn is defeated, reduce coordinated Thrawn pressure but keep post-Endor Imperial instability and future Imperial threats available.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Thrawn Defeated.
Respect story-position bounds from Thrawn Defeated to Thrawn Defeated.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

content.fact: Rukh kills Thrawn after Noghri loyalty turns, ending the Grand Admiral’s campaign at its decisive moment.
content.injection: At the end of the Thrawn campaign, Thrawn’s fall should connect to his exploitation of the Noghri, not simple battlefield incompetence.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Thrawn Defeated.
